result:
The fix is complete. Here's a summary of the changes:

## Changes Made

### 1. `ktlint-rule-engine-core/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/core/api/ElementType.kt` (line 216)
Added `RANGE_UNTIL` element type constant that maps to `KtTokens.RANGE_UNTIL` (the `..<` operator token), right after the existing `RANGE` entry.

### 2. `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/SpacingAroundRangeOperatorRule.kt`
- Added import for `RANGE_UNTIL`
- Extended the condition on line 18 from `node.elementType == RANGE` to `node.elementType == RANGE || node.elementType == RANGE_UNTIL` so the rule now handles both `..` and `..<` operators
- Made the error messages dynamic by using `node.text` instead of hardcoded `".."`, so error messages correctly show `"..<"` for the range-until operator

### Root Cause
The `SpacingAroundRangeOperatorRule` only checked for `RANGE` (`..`) element type and had no awareness of `RANGE_UNTIL` (`..<`). The `..<` operator was introduced in Kotlin 1.7.20 and stabilized in 1.8.0, but `KtTokens.RANGE_UNTIL` was available in the compiler (1.8.10) used by this project — it just wasn't exposed in ktlint's `ElementType` wrapper or handled by the spacing rule.
